section_name: Violations of Regulations or Prohibitions Imposed by Populous Counties on Off-premise Portable Signs
chapter_title: Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
text:
(a) If a county adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) , the attorney representing the county in district court may seek injunctive relief to prevent the violation or threatened violation of the prohibition or regulation.

(b) The commissioners court of a county that adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) by order may define an offense for the violation of the prohibition or regulation.

(c) An offense defined by a commissioners court under Subsection (b) is a Class C misdemeanor.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 394. Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
##### § 394.084 — Violations of Regulations or Prohibitions Imposed by Populous Counties on Off-premise Portable Signs

(a) If a county adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) , the attorney representing the county in district court may seek injunctive relief to prevent the violation or threatened violation of the prohibition or regulation.

(b) The commissioners court of a county that adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) by order may define an offense for the violation of the prohibition or regulation.

(c) An offense defined by a commissioners court under Subsection (b) is a Class C misdemeanor.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The commissioners court of a county that adopts a prohibition or regulation under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) by order may define an offense for the violation of the prohibition or regulation.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: An offense defined by a commissioners court under Subsection (b) is a Class C misdemeanor.
